text.title: Solo Affiliate Program Core Rules
text.summary: Five hard rules for a solo founder running an affiliate program without a marketing team or fraud-prevention vendor.

rule.statement: If your payment platform has a built-in affiliate module (Lemon Squeezy, Gumroad, Paddle), use it. Don't add Rewardful or a custom solution unless the native option blocks a specific requirement (multi-tier commissions, custom payout currencies, niche-specific reporting).

rationale.source: solo-founder time-budget reality; LS / Gumroad affiliate adoption data 2024

rule.rationale: Custom affiliate tooling costs 20-40 hours setup + ongoing maintenance. LS native costs 30 minutes. For < 50 affiliates the native option is always net-positive. Graduating later to Rewardful is cheap once you have a working program.

rule.statement: Affiliate commission % must leave you a positive gross margin AFTER payment-processor fees + commission + refund reserve. Formula: max_commission = gross_margin - processor_fee - refund_reserve - operating_margin. Use compute-commission-from-margin.py.
rule.rationale: Indie hackers routinely pick "30% recurring" because they saw it elsewhere, without computing margins. At 70% gross margin and 5% refund reserve and 4% Stripe fee, 30% commission leaves 31% operating margin — fine. At 50% margin and 30% commission, you're underwater. The formula must precede the policy.

rule.statement: Every program has 4 fraud guards: (1) anti-self-referral (block payouts when affiliate's IP / email / payment-method matches the converted customer), (2) cookie window cap (≤ 60 days), (3) payout-hold (≥ refund_window + 7 days before commission paid), (4) review queue for new affiliates' first 3 conversions.

rationale.source: affiliate fraud reports - Forrester 2024 (~10% fraud rate in unaudited programs); Rewardful fraud-prevention guides

rule.rationale: Without guards, fraud eats 10-30% of payouts. Self-referral is the #1 abuse (~6% of solo-program payouts). Cookie window caps prevent "I clicked once 2 years ago" attribution. Payout hold lets refunds clawback before money leaves. New-affiliate review catches the bad-actor cluster.

rule.statement: If a referred customer refunds within your refund window, the affiliate commission is clawed back. This is written into the affiliate terms BEFORE the affiliate signs up. No retroactive policy changes.

rationale.source: LS / Rewardful default behavior; legal contract enforceability

rule.rationale: Affiliates who don't expect clawback dispute it loudly. The terms page must specify clawback explicitly. Retroactive policy = lost affiliates AND lost legal standing. The payout-hold (r3.3) makes clawback frictionless: nothing has been paid yet.

rule.statement: Every program has a public terms page (or a sign-up agreement) covering: commission %, cookie window, payout schedule, payout minimum, clawback policy, prohibited promotion (no PPC bidding on your brand, no incentivized clicks, no email spam), and an FTC-style disclosure requirement for affiliates to disclose their relationship.

rationale.source: FTC 16 CFR Part 255 (endorsement guides); EU UCPD 2005/29/EC

rule.rationale: FTC fines for non-disclosure run $5k-$50k per violation in the US. Solo founder is legally responsible for affiliate disclosure. The terms page is the contract that lets you terminate non-compliant affiliates. EU follows similar disclosure law via UCPD.

example.title: Commission compute respecting margins
example.code:
Product: SaaS at $29/mo Gross margin (after hosting + support proportional): 76% = $22.04 Stripe fee: 2.9% + 30¢ on $29 = $1.14 Refund reserve (5% of MRR refunds within window): = $1.10 Operating margin target (you keep): ≥ 35% = $7.65 Available for commission: $22.04 - $1.14 - $1.10 - $7.65 = $12.15 Commission as % of $29: = 41.9%
Set commission at 30% recurring (safe headroom). Set first-month bonus at 40% (acquisition push).
example.note: Illustrates r2: formula-driven not vibes-driven commission setting.

example.title: Commission set by aspiration
example.code:
"I saw a competitor offers 50% recurring so I'll match it."
Product: $19/mo, gross margin 55% = $10.45 50% commission = $9.50 Stripe fee + refund reserve = $1.74 Operating margin: -$0.79 per referred sale, EVERY MONTH.
Problem: each affiliate sale is a loss. Volume of affiliate sales is the fastest way to bankrupt the business. Repair: max safe commission at this margin is ~20% recurring or 30% first-month-only. Match competitor only after raising prices or cutting COGS.
example.note: The "match competitor" pattern is the canonical margin-killer.
